Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Hold on to your sewing chairs! The Midnight Quilt Show is going bold, as in late-night foundation paper-piecing bold. (Angela makes it easy!)
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to build your own backyard barbeque cart for your next cookout. Learn the best types of wood to use and how to assemble your cart to achieve a professional look. George also discusses which glue and fasteners to use for a barbeque cart that will withstand the elements.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Backyard Barbecue Cart

Video.summary: Give a new twist to traditional art forms. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes a trinket box inspired by a popular video game. Then, she makes a friendly turtle using the ancient art of mosaic. Candie Cooper creates pop art with polka dots in the style of the artist Kusama, and Jenny coils a rag basket with a touch of denim.
Video.title: Old is New

Video.summary: -Cordless drills are a staple in many woodworking shops and deserve a dedicated storage stand. George Vondriska shows you how to make your own cordless drill stand, fully equipped with a power strip for easy charging, plus storage drawers for tips and bits. This project is great for practicing biscuit joinery and hand tool work.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Cordless Drill Stand

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The simple wooden spatula is an essential cooking tool that adds a handmade touch to any kitchen. George Vondriska demonstrates how to make your own wooden spatula in a few easy steps. This easy project makes a great gift for any home chef.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Handmade Wooden Spatula

Video.summary: Folk art is the essence of a culture. Painter and maker Mystele Kirkeeng paints abstract folk art faces and figures. Tool expert Joe Rotella uses baker's twine to weave new art on a tin tile. Host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer draws repeating shapes to create a meditative mandala. Jane Dunnewold shares another artistic perspective.
Video.title: Folk Art

Video.summary: Renovate the surface of your pages. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer adds depth to her designs with amazing shrinking embellishments. May Flaum creates dynamic designs with misty effects using scraps. Ashley Horton has a tip for storing tiny letters and embellishments. Cheryl Boglioli makes 3-D designs with fringed fabric and felt.
Video.title: Episode 6: DIY Embellishments

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Angela raids her husband's closet to stitch up a special quilted Father's Day gift. This T-shirt quilt for Jeremy isn't just any old T-shirt quilt; it's a very cool design that uses big flying geese to frame the blocks.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The Industrial Revolution was in full swing and women ventured beyond their homes into the workplace and politics. Angela Wolf creates a luxurious kimono duster inspired by those worn over long, full skirts and dresses. Kathy McMakin uses today's machines to create a precious heirloom baby bonnet you'll treasure.
Video.title: The New Century Begins - The 1900's

Video.summary: Think small, look at the bits and pieces that make up modern quilting. Laura Hartrich discusses three methods for creating quilt labels - an integral part of every quilt. Audrey Esarey demonstrates paper piecing – the modern way. Eliane Bergmann is all about stitch lengths in the machine tip segment.
Video.title: Bits and Pieces

Video.summary: On the season finale of Season 6 of the Midnight Quilt Show, follow along as Angela shares her clever take on a braided quilt pattern she created herself!
Video.title: Braided Star Quilt

Video.summary: Angela shows how to keep everything in order to make piecing a breeze.
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Keeping Blocks Together

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Keeping Blocks Together
Bonus: Tips for Keeping Blocks Together
Bonus: Tips for Keeping Blocks Together

Video.summary: This four-patch variation with cornerstones is the talk of the town! Watch Angela as she plays with color and dot-to-dot quilting.
Video.title: Town Square

Video.summary: Ready to find your center? Tonight, Angela becomes one with the piecing as she makes a stress-relieving half-square triangle quilt.
Video.title: Stress Relieving Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: Are you a "slow and steady" quilter or more of a "forget perfect, finish fast" quilter like Angela? Tonight, she uses templates and block piecing with LOTS of triangles to create an out-of-this-world ombré quilt. Plus, she shares some handy tips for quilting with rulers.
Video.title: Colorful Geometric Quilt

Video.summary: Did you know that painter's tape can make quilting a bit easier? Angela shares some (unconventional) ways to use it as she creates a striking stardust quilt. Plus, watch the free bonus video to get an extra painter's tape hack for sewing half-square triangles.
Video.title: Spring Stardust Quilt

Video.summary: If you're new to FMQ, quilting stencils are a great way to start!
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Video.summary: Angela's been practicing her magic tricks! Tonight, she amazes with a magnificent optical illusion quilt. Watch as she uses solid pre-cuts to magically create her star blocks.
Video.title: Optical Illusion Star Quilt

Video.summary: Angela shares her favorite way to make a quilt sandwich.
Video.title: Bonus: All About the Baste

Video.summary: Angela ponders on her new year's resolutions as she builds a curved log cabin quilt using ombré fabric.
Video.title: Curved Log Cabin Quilt

Video.summary: Fashion rules went out the window in the 1970's. The t-shirt made the statement for day wear. Joanne Banko creates a custom embroidered t-shirt for a special cause. Jumpsuits were all the rage and are in again. Angela Wolf shows how to create a jumpsuit by combining existing pant and top patterns.
Video.title: Fashion Free-for-all - 1970's

Video.summary: Excursions of all kinds whether online, in books or via photos can help us develop our sense of place. Today's designers have 2 different journeys for their inspiration. Lindsay Burke looks to a favorite ocean front locale to create her piece from resin. Then Kate Richbourg shares a design she calls Odyssey using waxed linen and beads for a very international vibe.
Video.title: Excursions

Video.summary: Today's theme is shape it. Use these techniques to form shapes that give structure and interest to your jewelry projects. Stitch wire and bead chandelier earrings, make gum drop- bead embroidered mandalas with Jennifer Van Benschoten, form a no fail soldered bangle bracelet with Kate Richbourg, and then, sculpt a beaded collar.
Video.title: Shape It

Video.summary: Wire up your jewelry-making routine. Designer Wyatt White coaches you as he uses braided wire to make a trendy bangle bracelet. In the workout, he adds energy and interest to a stylish cuff with beads. He winds down with a cool tool for weaving colorful wire bracelets.
Video.title: Wired Routines

Video.summary: Words and lettering can add an interesting element to your scrapbooking. First, May Flaum creates a canvas with a stamped bird and word cut outs. Next, Julie has some tips for hand lettering. Then, Dina Wakley demonstrates paint and ink techniques using a stencil with gesso and pattern resist. Julie has a last tip for embellishing and using letter stickers.
Video.title: Episode 8: Words and Lettering

Video.summary: The ladies are going big by thinking small! From compact cables to cute mini skeins, this one's all about the tiny treasures that keep those needles clicking.
Video.title: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Image.alt: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Video.summary: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!
Video.title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Image.alt: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Video.summary: Animals come in endless varieties. Inspired by ocean creatures, Jenny Barnett-Rohrs creates a sea-life totem. Candy Cooper crafts a piggy bank that holds bills and coins. Artist Franz Spohn engineers animals from all directions. Jenny crafts loony llamas from pool noodles.
Video.title: Animals

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Angela is back for Season 5 of the Midnight Quilt Show! Today she is making the Arrows Quilt by Krystal Jakelwicz, and incorporating a little love note into her machine quilting.
Video.title: Episode 1
